name: Xata (Serverless Database with Search)
pricing_notes: Free tier (15GB, 75M AI requests); Pro from $20/mo

auth.notes: API Key per database; TypeScript SDK auto-configured from .env

performance.notes: Postgres + Elasticsearch under the hood; search latency higher than pure DB

agent_readiness.notes: Serverless Postgres + Elasticsearch hybrid. TypeScript SDK with generated types from schema. xata.db.posts.filter().getMany(). Full-text search with xata.db.posts.search('query'). Branching (like git for databases). Ask AI built-in (pgvector). Schema migrations via web UI or CLI. Automatic backups. Vercel/Netlify integrations. No connection pooling needed. Suitable for Next.js/Vercel stack projects wanting search without Algolia. Moving from proprietary to pure Postgres (2025).

gotchas: Transitioning away from proprietary DB to pure Postgres (architecture shift)
Search latency higher than pure Postgres queries
Smaller community than Neon or PlanetScale
best_when: Vercel/Next.js projects needing Postgres + search together
Startups wanting serverless DB with built-in full-text search
Projects avoiding Algolia/Typesense complexity for basic search
Teams wanting database branching for schema changes
avoid_when: Projects needing mature PostgreSQL ecosystem (Neon or Supabase better)
Complex SQL queries (use Neon or Supabase directly)
summary: Xata is a serverless database combining Postgres + Elasticsearch. TypeScript SDK with generated types. Built-in full-text search, database branching, automatic backups, AI ask feature. No connection pooling needed. Transitioning to pure Postgres backend. Best for Vercel/Next.js projects needing DB + search without separate services. Neon or Supabase for pure Postgres needs.
